     The Numbers Business Report

Life (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $30,234,022Details
International Box Office $71,581,638
Worldwide Box Office $101,815,660
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,889,425
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,389,524
Total $10,278,949
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story of the six-member crew of the International Space Station that is on the cutting edge of one of the most important discoveries in human history: the first evidence of extraterrestrial life on Mars. As the crew begins to conduct research, their methods end up having unintended consequences and the life form proves more intelligent than anyone ever expected.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,501,936 (41.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.42 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$58,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,146 opening theaters/3,146 max. theaters, 3.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $40,312,029

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 24th, 2017 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: March 22nd, 2017 (Wide) (Sweden)
March 22nd, 2017 (Wide) (Indonesia)
March 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (Germany)
March 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (Bahrain)
March 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (Bolivia)
Video Release: June 2nd, 2017 by Columbia Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, some sci-fi violence and terror.
(Rating bulletin 2467 (Cert #50578), 3/15/2017)
Running Time: 103 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: One Location
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Outer Space, Space Station
Plot point: Alien Encounters, Trapped
Subgenre: Isolation Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Skydance Productions
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

As the crew begins to conduct research, their methods end up having unintended consequences and the life form proves more intelligent than anyone ever expected.
